title: Listen and Choose a Response — Travel & Transportation

questions.stem: Is there a campus shuttle to the airport during winter break?

options.A: Yes, we run a shuttle every two hours. You need to sign up in advance.
options.B: Airport shuttles are usually shared rides.
options.C: I'm taking a taxi to the airport.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about transportation availability, so confirming the service and explaining the booking process is helpful.

questions.context: Asking about directions to a building
questions.stem: Excuse me, can you tell me how to get to the engineering building from here?

options.A: Sure, go straight past the fountain, then take the second left.
options.B: The engineering building has six floors.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking for directions, so providing clear, step-by-step instructions is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: Where can I park my bike on campus? I don't want it to get towed.

options.A: There are bike racks in front of every major building. Just avoid locking to railings.
options.B: Biking is a great way to get around campus.
options.C: I walk to class every day.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about proper bike parking, so explaining where racks are and what to avoid is directly helpful.

questions.stem: The traffic on Main Street was terrible this morning. I was stuck for forty minutes.

options.A: Tell me about it. There's construction near the campus entrance.
options.B: Traffic jams are measured by vehicle density.
options.C: I take the back roads to avoid traffic.

questions.explanation: The speaker is complaining about traffic, so showing shared understanding and providing context is the natural empathetic response.

questions.stem: Does the university have a carpool program for students who commute?

options.A: Yes, you can register on the sustainability portal to find carpool partners.
options.B: Carpooling reduces carbon emissions from commuting.
options.C: I drive alone to campus every day.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about a specific program, so confirming its existence and explaining how to access it is helpful.

questions.stem: Is there a taxi stand near the dorms, or should I call one in advance?

options.A: It's better to call or use a ride-sharing app. Taxis rarely wait near the dorms.
options.B: Taxis charge by mileage and time.

questions.explanation: The speaker is asking about local transportation logistics, so providing practical advice about what works best is helpful.

questions.stem: My bike has a flat tire and I need to get to class in fifteen minutes.

options.A: You can borrow my bike. I don't have class until noon.
options.B: Flat tires can be fixed with a repair kit.
options.C: I walk to class, so I don't need a bike.

questions.explanation: The speaker has a practical problem and a time constraint, so offering a generous immediate solution is the most helpful response.

questions.stem: I'm studying abroad next semester. Do I need any special travel documents?

options.A: Yes, make sure your passport is valid and check if you need a student visa.
options.B: Study abroad programs offer unique cultural experiences.
options.C: I've always wanted to study abroad.

questions.explanation: The speaker is preparing for international travel and asking about requirements, so providing key documentation advice is helpful.
